english_name: The First Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Corinthians

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic nos existimet homo ut ministros Christi, et dispensatores mysteriorum Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως ἡμᾶς λογιζέσθω ἄνθρωπος ὡς ὑπηρέτας χριστοῦ καὶ οἰκονόμους μυστηρίων θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Let a man so account of us, as of the ministers of Christ, and stewards of the mysteries of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hic jam quæritur inter dispensatores ut fidelis quis inveniatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅ δὲ λοιπὸν ζητεῖται ἐν τοῖς οἰκονόμοις ἵνα πιστός τις εὑρεθῇ
verses.king_james: Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mihi autem pro minimo est ut a vobis judicer, aut ab humano die: sed neque meipsum judico.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐμοὶ δὲ εἰς ἐλάχιστόν ἐστιν ἵνα ὑφ᾽ ὑμῶν ἀνακριθῶ ἢ ὑπὸ ἀνθρωπίνης ἡμέρας ἀλλ᾽ οὐδὲ ἐμαυτὸν ἀνακρίνω
verses.king_james: But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged of you, or of man's judgment: yea, I judge not mine own self.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nihil enim mihi conscius sum, sed non in hoc justificatus sum: qui autem judicat me, Dominus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδὲν γὰρ ἐμαυτῷ σύνοιδα ἀλλ᾽ οὐκ ἐν τούτῳ δεδικαίωμαι ὁ δὲ ἀνακρίνων με κύριός ἐστιν
verses.king_james: For I know nothing by myself; yet am I not hereby justified: but he that judgeth me is the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque nolite ante tempus judicare, quoadusque veniat Dominus: qui et illuminabit abscondita tenebrarum, et manifestabit consilia cordium: et tunc laus erit unicuique a Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε μὴ πρὸ καιροῦ τι κρίνετε ἕως ἂν ἔλθῃ ὁ κύριος ὃς καὶ φωτίσει τὰ κρυπτὰ τοῦ σκότους καὶ φανερώσει τὰς βουλὰς τῶν καρδιῶν καὶ τότε ὁ ἔπαινος γενήσεται ἑκάστῳ ἀπὸ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Therefore judge nothing before the time, until the Lord come, who both will bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and will make manifest the counsels of the hearts: and then shall every man have praise of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc autem, fratres, transfiguravi in me et Apollo, propter vos: ut in nobis discatis, ne supra quam scriptum est, unus adversus alterum infletur pro alio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα δέ ἀδελφοί μετεσχημάτισα εἰς ἐμαυτὸν καὶ ἀπολλῶ δι᾽ ὑμᾶς ἵνα ἐν ἡμῖν μάθητε τὸ μὴ ὑπὲρ ὃ γέγραπται φρονεῖν, ἵνα μὴ εἷς ὑπὲρ τοῦ ἑνὸς φυσιοῦσθε κατὰ τοῦ ἑτέρου
verses.king_james: And these things, brethren, I have in a figure transferred to myself and *to* Apollos for your sakes; that ye might learn in us not to think *of men* above that which is written, that no one of you be puffed up for one against another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis enim te discernit? quid autem habes quod non accepisti? si autem accepisti, quid gloriaris quasi non acceperis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίς γάρ σε διακρίνει τί δὲ ἔχεις ὃ οὐκ ἔλαβες εἰ δὲ καὶ ἔλαβες τί καυχᾶσαι ὡς μὴ λαβών
verses.king_james: For who maketh thee to differ *from another*? and what hast thou that thou didst not receive? now if thou didst receive *it*, why dost thou glory, as if thou hadst not received *it* ?

verses.latin_vulgate: Jam saturati estis, jam divites facti estis: sine nobis regnatis: et utinam regnetis, ut et nos vobiscum regnemus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἤδη κεκορεσμένοι ἐστέ ἤδη ἐπλουτήσατε χωρὶς ἡμῶν ἐβασιλεύσατε καὶ ὄφελόν γε ἐβασιλεύσατε ἵνα καὶ ἡμεῖς ὑμῖν συμβασιλεύσωμεν
verses.king_james: Now ye are full, now ye are rich, ye have reigned as kings without us: and I would to God ye did reign, that we also might reign with you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Puto enim quod Deus nos Apostolos novissimos ostendit, tamquam morti destinatos: quia spectaculum facti sumus mundo, et angelis, et hominibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δοκῶ γάρ ὅτι ὁ θεὸς ἡμᾶς τοὺς ἀποστόλους ἐσχάτους ἀπέδειξεν ὡς ἐπιθανατίους ὅτι θέατρον ἐγενήθημεν τῷ κόσμῳ καὶ ἀγγέλοις καὶ ἀνθρώποις
verses.king_james: For I think that God hath set forth us the apostles last, as it were appointed to death: for we are made a spectacle unto the world, and to angels, and to men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nos stulti propter Christum, vos autem prudentes in Christo: nos infirmi, vos autem fortes: vos nobiles, nos autem ignobiles.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡμεῖς μωροὶ διὰ χριστόν ὑμεῖς δὲ φρόνιμοι ἐν χριστῷ ἡμεῖς ἀσθενεῖς ὑμεῖς δὲ ἰσχυροί ὑμεῖς ἔνδοξοι ἡμεῖς δὲ ἄτιμοι
verses.king_james: We *are* fools for Christ's sake, but ye *are* wise in Christ; we *are* weak, but ye *are* strong; ye *are* honourable, but we *are* despised.

verses.latin_vulgate: Usque in hanc horam et esurimus, et sitimus, et nudi sumus, et colaphis cædimur, et instabiles sumus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄχρι τῆς ἄρτι ὥρας καὶ πεινῶμεν καὶ διψῶμεν καὶ γυμνητεύομεν, καὶ κολαφιζόμεθα καὶ ἀστατοῦμεν
verses.king_james: Even unto this present hour we both hunger, and thirst, and are naked, and are buffeted, and have no certain dwellingplace;

verses.latin_vulgate: et laboramus operantes manibus nostris: maledicimur, et benedicimus: persecutionem patimur, et sustinemus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ κοπιῶμεν ἐργαζόμενοι ταῖς ἰδίαις χερσίν λοιδορούμενοι εὐλογοῦμεν διωκόμενοι ἀνεχόμεθα
verses.king_james: And labour, working with our own hands: being reviled, we bless; being persecuted, we suffer it:

verses.latin_vulgate: blasphemamur, et obsecramus: tamquam purgamenta hujus mundi facti sumus, omnium peripsema usque adhuc.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: βλασφημούμενοι παρακαλοῦμεν ὡς περικαθάρματα τοῦ κόσμου ἐγενήθημεν πάντων περίψημα ἕως ἄρτι
verses.king_james: Being defamed, we intreat: we are made as the filth of the world, *and are* the offscouring of all things unto this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non ut confundam vos, hæc scribo, sed ut filios meos carissimos moneo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἐντρέπων ὑμᾶς γράφω ταῦτα ἀλλ᾽ ὡς τέκνα μου ἀγαπητὰ νουθετῶ
verses.king_james: I write not these things to shame you, but as my beloved sons I warn *you*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam si decem millia pædagogorum habeatis in Christo, sed non multos patres. Nam in Christo Jesu per Evangelium ego vos genui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν γὰρ μυρίους παιδαγωγοὺς ἔχητε ἐν χριστῷ ἀλλ᾽ οὐ πολλοὺς πατέρας ἐν γὰρ χριστῷ ἰησοῦ διὰ τοῦ εὐαγγελίου ἐγὼ ὑμᾶς ἐγέννησα
verses.king_james: For though ye have ten thousand instructors in Christ, yet *have ye* not many fathers: for in Christ Jesus I have begotten you through the gospel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rogo ergo vos, imitatores mei estote, sicut et ego Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παρακαλῶ οὖν ὑμᾶς μιμηταί μου γίνεσθε
verses.king_james: Wherefore I beseech you, be ye followers of me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo misi ad vos Timotheum, qui est filius meus carissimus, et fidelis in Domino: qui vos commonefaciet vias meas, quæ sunt in Christo Jesu, sicut ubique in omni ecclesia doceo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτο ἔπεμψα ὑμῖν τιμόθεον ὅς ἐστίν τέκνον μου ἀγαπητὸν καὶ πιστὸν ἐν κυρίῳ ὃς ὑμᾶς ἀναμνήσει τὰς ὁδούς μου τὰς ἐν χριστῷ καθὼς πανταχοῦ ἐν πάσῃ ἐκκλησίᾳ διδάσκω
verses.king_james: For this cause have I sent unto you Timotheus, who is my beloved son, and faithful in the Lord, who shall bring you into remembrance of my ways which be in Christ, as I teach every where in every church.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tamquam non venturus sim ad vos, sic inflati sunt quidam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς μὴ ἐρχομένου δέ μου πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἐφυσιώθησάν τινες
verses.king_james: Now some are puffed up, as though I would not come to you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Veniam autem ad vos cito, si Dominus voluerit: et cognoscam non sermonem eorum qui inflati sunt, sed virtutem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλεύσομαι δὲ ταχέως πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἐὰν ὁ κύριος θελήσῃ καὶ γνώσομαι οὐ τὸν λόγον τῶν πεφυσιωμένων ἀλλὰ τὴν δύναμιν
verses.king_james: But I will come to you shortly, if the Lord will, and will know, not the speech of them which are puffed up, but the power.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim in sermone est regnum Dei, sed in virtute.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ ἐν λόγῳ ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ ἀλλ᾽ ἐν δυνάμει
verses.king_james: For the kingdom of God *is* not in word, but in power.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid vultis? in virga veniam ad vos, an in caritate, et spiritu mansuetudinis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί θέλετε ἐν ῥάβδῳ ἔλθω πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἢ ἐν ἀγάπῃ πνεύματί τε πρᾳότητος
verses.king_james: What will ye? shall I come unto you with a rod, or in love, and *in* the spirit of meekness?
